ocr.text: In the analysis of Coupang's Seller onboarding growth metrics from 2020 to 2022, the total sellers increased from 5,000 in 2020
to 6,500 in 2021, and further to 8,000 in 2022, while new sellers rose from 1,500 to 1,800 and then to 2,200, reflecting a growth
rate that decreased from 30% in 2020 to 28% in 2021 and 23% in 2022, alongside a retention rate consistently at 85% for 2020
and 80% for 2021, improving slightly to 82% in 2022. The onboarding metrics reveal completed onboarding rising from 4,000
in 2020 to 5,200 in 2021, and reaching 6,500 in 2022, with the time to onboard decreasing from 30 days to 27 days, and then to
25 days, while the satisfaction index improved from 75% to 80% and finally to 82%.
SellerMetrics  2020  2021
TotalSellers  NewSellers  GrowthRate  [HIDDEN]
OnboardingMetrics  CompletedOnboarding  TimetoOnboard  SatisfactionIndex  SupportRequests  ConversionRate  RevenuePerSeller

GrowthRate  30%  28%  23%  RetentionRate  85%  80%  82%  OnboardingCompleted  4,000  5,200  6,500  TimetoOnboard  30days
27days  25days  SatisfactionIndex  75%  80%  82%  SupportRequests  300  250  200  ConversionRate  12%  14%  15%
RevenuePerSeller  $20,000  $22,000  $24,000  TotalSellers  5,000  6,500  8,000  NewSellers  1,500  1,800  2,200  GrowthRate  30%
Support requests decreased from 300 in 2020 to 250 in 2021 and further to 200 in 2022. The conversion rate showed an upward
trend from 12% to 14% and then to 15%, while revenue per seller also increased from $20,000 in 2020 to $22,000 in 2021, and
$24,000 in 2022. This data illustrates a clear trajectory of growth in seller onboarding efficiency and satisfaction, despite a
gradual decline in the growth rate of new sellers.
